items.title: Hong Kong’s office market: Central booms, but noncore areas struggle
items.summary:
Hong Kong’s office market is becoming increasingly divided, with demand returning to prime business districts while noncore areas remain weighed down by weak leasing activity and scarce buyers, analysts say.
The reluctance of banks to finance commercial-property purchases has reinforced the split, allowing cash-rich owner-occupiers to acquire discounted offices in traditional commercial districts

items.title: Chinese memory giant CXMT oversubscribed 212 times in mega Shanghai IPO

items.title: China memory-chip maker CXMT set for mega IPO
items.summary:
Subscriptions began on Thursday for shares in memory-chip maker ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT) ahead of its listing on Shanghai’s Star Market in what could be mainland China’s second biggest initial public offering.
With pricing set at 8.66 yuan (US$1.28) per share, the IPO of nearly 6.7 billion shares will raise gross proceeds of 57.9 billion yuan (US$8.5 billion).

items.title: What does South Korea’s Kospi chaos mean for China’s markets?
items.summary:
The volatile state of South Korean stocks is reminiscent for some investors of China’s rapid boom-to-bust cycle a decade ago, analysts say, when leveraged trading fuelled a meltdown that eventually wiped US$5 trillion from the market within months.
